name: org.machanism.machai:machai-cli
description:
Machai CLI is a Java (Spring Boot + Spring Shell) command-line application that provides an interactive shell for the Machanism Machai ecosystem. It orchestrates AI-assisted workflows around project/library metadata generation (bindex), metadata registration to an external registry service, semantic search ("pick") for discovering relevant libraries, and AI-driven project assembly/scaffolding. It also integrates the Machai Ghostwriter processors to scan documents/source code and produce refactoring guidance, and provides an interactive "Act mode" to execute predefined actions/prompts.

Key capabilities:
- Persistent configuration management via machai.properties using org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator (org.machanism.machai.cli.ConfigCommand). Common defaults include project directory (ProjectLayout.PROJECT_DIR_PROP_NAME), Ghostwriter scan directory (Ghostwriter.GW_SCAN_DIR_PROP_NAME), GenAI provider/model (Ghostwriter.GW_MODEL_PROP_NAME / BindexCreator.MODEL_PROP_NAME), and input logging (Genai.LOG_INPUTS_PROP_NAME).
- Bindex generation: org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand.bindex delegates to org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexCreator to scan a project directory and generate bindex metadata; supports update mode and configurable GenAI provider/model.
- Bindex registration: org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand.register delegates to org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexRegister to upload/register generated bindex metadata into an external registry URL; supports update mode and configurable model.
- Ghostwriter guidance pipeline: org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand.gw delegates to org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor to scan directories/files, apply system instructions and default guidance prompts (resolved from configuration or provided interactively), run concurrently with a configurable thread count, support exclude lists, and optionally log LLM request inputs.
- Act mode: org.machanism.machai.cli.ActCommand.act delegates to org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.ActProcessor to scan documents under a configured scan directory (falling back to the project directory) and execute an interactive predefined action/prompt; builds a default prompt from provided command words and supports optional LLM input logging.
- Workspace cleanup: org.machanism.machai.cli.CleanCommand.clean recursively removes Machai temporary directories named .machai from a directory tree.

Runtime/integration details:
- Spring Boot entry point: org.machanism.machai.cli.MachaiCLI. On startup it loads additional JVM system properties from machai.properties, or from a file specified via -Dconfig=/path/to/file, then starts Spring Shell.
- Logging: SLF4J + Logback configuration outputs INFO+ to console and DEBUG+ to rolling log files under ${machai.home:-.}/logs.

Primary dependencies/integrations: machai bindex-core, machai ghostwriter, Spring Shell, and the Machai GenAI provider abstraction (org.machanism.machai.ai.*). This artifact is intended for local developer workstations and CI/CD scripting where interactive or scripted metadata generation, semantic discovery, and AI-assisted project assembly/refactoring workflows are needed.

constructors.description: Main entry point for starting the Machai CLI. Bootstraps Spring Boot and Spring Shell. On startup it loads additional JVM system properties from a machai.properties file, or from a file path specified by the -Dconfig system property, before starting the application.

examples.description: Starts the interactive Machai CLI shell using machai.properties if present.

constructors.description: Creates the Ghostwriter guidance command. A JLine LineReader is injected (typically by Spring Shell) and used to prompt for instructions/guidance interactively when CLI options are empty.

examples.snippet:
org.jline.reader.LineReader reader = null;
org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand(reader);
examples.description: Spring Shell typically provides the LineReader; the command uses it to request missing prompt inputs in interactive mode.

features.name: gw
features.signature: void gw(int threads, java.lang.String model, java.lang.String instructions, java.lang.String guidance, java.lang.String excludes, java.lang.Boolean logInputs, java.io.File projectDir, java.lang.String[] scanDirs)
features.description: Scans and processes directories or files using the Ghostwriter guidance pipeline. Resolves defaults (project directory, model, instructions, guidance, logging) from machai.properties via ConfigCommand. Supports concurrency (threads), exclude lists, multiple scan directories, interactive prompt entry for empty instructions/guidance, and optional logging of LLM request inputs.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand((org.jline.reader.LineReader) null);
cmd.gw(4, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", "You are a strict code reviewer", "Refactor for clarity", "target,.git", java.lang.Boolean.TRUE, new java.io.File("."), new java.lang.String[]{"./src"});
examples.description: Runs Ghostwriter guidance processing on ./src using 4 threads, excluding target and .git, and logging LLM request inputs.

features.name: act
features.description: Runs Ghostwriter "Act mode" interactively using org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.ActProcessor. Joins provided words into a default prompt, resolves the project directory and model from persisted configuration, determines the scan directory from configuration (falling back to the project directory), scans documents, and executes the action/prompt. Supports optional logging of LLM request inputs.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.ActCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.ActCommand();
cmd.act(new java.lang.String[]{"commit", "and", "push"});
examples.description: Executes Act mode using the default project directory and model, with default prompt "commit and push".

features.name: bindex
features.signature: void bindex(java.io.File dir, boolean update, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Generates bindex metadata files for a project directory by scanning sources and project descriptors. Delegates to org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexCreator; supports update mode (update previously saved metadata) and configurable GenAI model selection (explicit option or configured default).

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand();
cmd.bindex(new java.io.File("./my-project"), false, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Scans ./my-project and generates bindex metadata using the specified model.

features.name: register
features.signature: void register(java.io.File dir, java.lang.String registerUrl, boolean update, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Registers (uploads) previously generated bindex metadata from a project directory to an external registry endpoint. Delegates to org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexRegister; supports update mode and configurable GenAI model selection.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand();
cmd.register(new java.io.File("./my-project"), "https://registry.example/api", true, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Uploads bindex metadata from ./my-project to the registry, updating existing entries.

features.name: set
features.signature: void set(java.lang.String key, java.lang.String value) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Sets or gets a persistent CLI configuration property stored in machai.properties using org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator. If value is null, prints the current property value; otherwise sets and saves it. Used to configure defaults for directories, models, and logging behavior.

examples.description: Configures the default GenAI model and default project directory in machai.properties.

features.name: clean
features.description: Recursively removes Machai temporary directories named .machai from a directory tree. Intended to clean intermediate artifacts produced by Machai workflows.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.CleanCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.CleanCommand();
cmd.clean(new java.io.File("./my-project"));
